_meta.purpose: Phase 1 failure mode classification audit trail

failure_modes.1: Full concession — defender concedes outright ('The critic raises valid points...')
failure_modes.2: Partial concession — defender hedges rather than wins ('While the critic is partially correct...')
failure_modes.3: Weak rebuttal — defender argues but doesn't address the specific claim
failure_modes.4: Correct rebuttal, overridden — defender makes strong argument, adjudicator sides with critic anyway
failure_modes.5: Unfalsifiable critique — critic raises something that can't be rebutted ('could have selection bias')
failure_modes.6: Noise accumulation — critic raises minor nits, defender concedes some, adjudicator counts concessions as evidence

classification_key.runs_seen: number of runs read before classifying
classification_key.consensus: whether all 3 runs showed same mode (true/false/partial)

cases.domain: e-commerce search / IR
cases.failure_mode_label: Partial concession — cascading concessions to false premise
cases.key_evidence: Defender accepted critic's reframing of hypothesis ('does semantic encoding alone beat BM25') instead of defending stated hypothesis ('does full transformer system beat BM25'). Task prompt Section 4 explicitly calls BM25 a 'feature-light baseline' by design; Section 9 explicitly limits scope. Defender then partially conceded on all 4 critiques; Pass 2 aggregated concessions into critique_wins.

cases.domain: hospital readmission / EHR sequence modeling
cases.failure_mode_label: Cascading full concessions after accepting false premise
cases.key_evidence: Critic reframed 'sequence representation vs. tabular aggregation' as 'transformer vs. strongest possible tabular model (GBDT).' Hypothesis explicitly tests LR on aggregated features vs. transformer on raw event sequences — changing data format, not model class. Defenders conceded fully on GBDT critique across all 3 runs without challenging the premise. Same false-premise acceptance pattern as eval_scenario_707.

cases.domain: hospital readmission / CHF / EHR sequence modeling
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — counter-argument present in document, never deployed
cases.key_evidence: Issue 1 (patient assignment bias): Section 7 explicitly includes stratified analysis by utilization quartile to control for exactly this concern. Defenders conceded 'substantial flaw' across all 3 runs without pointing to Section 7. Issue 2 (information confound): false-premise attack — hypothesis explicitly tests full history vs. most-recent-encounter summary; RNN's additional information IS the temporal structure under test. Defenders failed to deploy existing design elements as rebuttal.

cases.domain: small-business loan default / credit risk
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — multiple modest technical-sounding critiques, partial concessions on each, aggregated to empirical_test_agreed
cases.key_evidence: Bidirectionality critique is noise: prediction at origination time uses full pre-origination window, no future leakage. Zero-padding asymmetry addressed in Section 8. Defenders couldn't fully dismiss individual concerns even though none were fatal, accumulated partial concessions prevented defense_wins. All 3 runs landed at empirical_test_agreed — defenders held the line better than Cases 1-3 but still couldn't claim exoneration.

cases.domain: retail card fraud detection / sequence vs. tabular
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — individually weak critiques, defenders couldn't dismiss any, concessions aggregated to critique_wins
cases.key_evidence: Feature/architecture conflation critique: embeddings are explicitly part of the hypothesis ('sequence model trained on transaction behavior AND merchant/category embeddings'); feature parity section acknowledges gap as 'feature enhancement not model advantage.' Velocity ambiguity and bidirectionality are implementation concerns, not design flaws. All critiques were individually defeatable — design had answers — defenders failed to deploy them. Baseline was already XGBoost. All 3 runs: critique_wins.

cases.domain: edtech / online learning dropout prediction
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — heterogeneous minor critiques across runs, partial concessions on each, aggregated to critique_wins
cases.key_evidence: Across runs, critics raised different sets of individually moderate concerns (censoring gap at log horizon, terminal dwell-time leakage, threshold portability, weak baseline, label boundary ambiguity). None is fatal in isolation and no single issue appears across all 3 runs as a consensus killer. Defenders conceded partially on every concern raised, and Pass 2 accumulated these concessions without being able to claim full exoneration. Correct rebuttal for the censoring gap: design applies a fixed 14-day observation window starting day 14, so any learner near the log horizon whose window is incomplete should simply be excluded — a straightforward addition the defender never proposed.

cases.domain: airline operations / flight delay forecasting
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — genuine design flaw (months 13-18 in-sample) that defender conceded without proposing available fix
cases.key_evidence: Section 7 of the task prompt explicitly designates months 13–18 as a 'secondary time-blocked validation' while the training set covers months 1–24 — months 13–18 are entirely within the training window, making any evaluation there in-sample. This is a genuine design error, not a false-premise critique. Defenders conceded fully in 2/3 runs and partially in run2. The design also leaves the 7-day-ahead prediction horizon underspecified relative to features that require near-departure data. case_valid=false because the months 13-18 secondary validation window is a real internal inconsistency in the case design itself.

cases.domain: industrial IoT / pump failure survival modeling
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — design specifies DeLong test for C-index (wrong test) and random CV within validation window (temporal leakage); defenders conceded both without proposing correct alternatives
cases.key_evidence: Section 5 of the task prompt explicitly specifies 'DeLong test, p < 0.05' as the significance criterion for comparing C-index values — DeLong is invalid for survival C-index (valid for AUC only). Section 6 specifies '5-fold cross-validation within the validation window' without enforcing temporal ordering, reintroducing leakage inconsistent with the temporal split rationale. Both are genuine design errors conceded fully or substantially by defenders in all 3 runs. The correct rebuttal would require fixing both: bootstrap or permutation test for C-index, and walk-forward CV within the validation window. case_valid=false because these are real specification errors, not critic overreach.

cases.domain: clinical / HF 30-day readmission survival modeling
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — competing-events censoring debate, ablation gap, facility drift; all 3 runs land at empirical_test_agreed
cases.key_evidence: The competing-events-as-censoring design choice is explicitly stated and justified in the task prompt (Section 1: 'reflecting the clinical use case'). The critic correctly identified that this violates independent censoring but the design's stated rationale — that the clinical question is about acute-care return, not competing events — is a defensible scope decision. Defenders partially conceded on calibration claims without pointing to the explicit clinical scope justification. The ablation gap (missing LR on full longitudinal features) is a real omission. Multiple partial concessions accumulated to empirical_test_agreed across all 3 runs without any single fatal flaw.

cases.domain: emergency department / multimodal triage admission prediction
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — CV leakage concern, frozen embedding quality, note-length leakage; multiple partial concessions across all 3 runs
cases.key_evidence: The 5-fold CV within the 6-month validation window is flagged as temporal leakage in all 3 runs — the design specifies '5-fold CV' for hyperparameter tuning within months 25-30 without explicitly enforcing temporal fold ordering. Defenders conceded this as a genuine ambiguity rather than deploying the available defense that the validation set is a contiguous future block and walk-forward CV is an easy fix. The frozen embedding critique (may produce poor representations of short triage notes) is unfalsifiable without running the experiment. Stratification concern is a false-premise attack: Section 2 explicitly stratifies 'within each temporal segment' not across segments. Defenders failed to deploy the stratification defense consistently.

cases.domain: enterprise security / vulnerability patch prediction
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — LM cutoff leakage risk, missing CVSS baseline, label confound; multiple partial concessions yield empirical_test_agreed or critique_wins
cases.key_evidence: Three distinct concerns emerged across runs: (1) pre-trained LM training cutoff unspecified (real but addressable specification gap — Section 3 says 'fixed pre-trained language model' without specifying cutoff); (2) CVSS-threshold-only ranking baseline absent (missing comparison, not a fatal flaw); (3) 14-day label conflates urgency with organizational patching velocity (unfalsifiable scope critique). Defenders fully or substantially conceded each in isolation. The design's temporal split and feature construction are sound; none of the critiques is individually fatal. Pass 2 aggregated partial concessions to empirical_test_agreed in 2/3 runs and critique_wins in 1/3.

cases.domain: enterprise email security / BEC and phishing transformer
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — design specifies training-only reputation fit but leaves graph context construction underspecified; defenders conceded both rather than citing design controls
cases.key_evidence: Section 3 of the task prompt explicitly states sender reputation is 'fit on training data only' — the reputation leakage critique is directly refuted by the design, yet defenders conceded it as a 'genuine gap' in all 3 runs. The graph context construction is genuinely underspecified (design says 'derived from communications prior to the email send time' but doesn't enforce per-email snapshots vs. a static training-period graph). Defenders correctly conceded the graph leakage risk but failed to distinguish this from the reputation concern that the design already handles. Mode 3 dominates: defender had the answer for reputation, didn't cite it; graph context is a real specification gap.

cases.domain: enterprise email security / phishing transformer vs. GBM
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — BPE vocabulary, ablation logic, sender novelty stratification; multiple partial concessions yield empirical_test_agreed across all 3 runs
cases.key_evidence: Section 3 of the task prompt explicitly specifies 'BPE vocabulary fit exclusively on the training set' — this is a deliberate design choice, not a flaw. Defenders partially conceded this as potentially harmful (novel phishing vocabulary fragmentation) rather than defending it as a controlled training-only pipeline choice. The ablation logic error (full concession in run2) and missing text-only comparison are real scope gaps. The sender novelty stratification is a missing analysis, not a design flaw. None of the issues is individually fatal; defenders accumulated partial concessions on each, and Pass 2 tallied these as insufficient to reach defense_wins in all 3 runs.

cases.domain: clinical NLP / radiology report NER transformer vs. CRF
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — underpowered stratification, exact span match asymmetry, MIMIC pre-training overlap; partial concessions on each accumulated to empirical_test_agreed or critique_wins
cases.key_evidence: Three concerns recur across runs: (1) stratified F1 at 2,000-3,000 reports is underpowered for rare modality strata (unfalsifiable without running sample size calculation — design is silent on this); (2) exact span match may asymmetrically penalize CRF vs. transformer (the design specifies 'entity-level F1' but does not pre-specify lenient-match as a secondary metric); (3) ClinicalBERT/BioBERT pre-training corpus may include source hospital data. Defenders partially conceded on all three without noting that the chrono split protects against drift or that the underpowering concern requires empirical evidence. No single critique is fatal but all three concessions accumulated to empirical_test_agreed in 2/3 runs.

cases.domain: enterprise cybersecurity / GNN vs. GBDT compromise detection
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — k-hop feature parity, entity-disjoint interpretation, negative contamination; multiple full concessions yield critique_wins in 2/3 runs
cases.key_evidence: Section 3 describes parallel tabular and graph feature streams: GBDT receives only the tabular stream while GNN receives graph structure including k-hop neighborhood flags. The design does NOT give GBDT access to the k-hop 'previously flagged' signal — this is a genuine feature parity problem that defenders correctly conceded (full concession in run2). The entity-disjoint test interpretation ambiguity (sound comparison diagnostic vs. inflated metric denominator) and 90-day clean-negative contamination are real but magnitude-uncertain. Defenders fully conceded k-hop parity in run2 and the label anchoring bias in run2, while run0 and run1 showed partial concessions. The accumulation of these concessions drove critique_wins in 2 of 3 runs.

cases.domain: cybersecurity email / spear-phishing detection
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — reputation freeze and multi-signal consensus cited in design, never deployed by defenders
cases.key_evidence: Section 3 explicitly freezes reputation scores at end-of-training and records label provenance for stratified analysis. Critics attack retroactive TI labeling and reputation snapshot design; defenders argue generically ('recovery paths exist') without citing Section 1's 7-day dwell + multi-signal consensus language or Section 3's frozen-snapshot wording. In run2, the reputation-freeze critique is conceded despite the design explicitly addressing it.

cases.domain: cybersecurity intrusion detection / sequence vs tabular
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — bidirectional look-ahead, host-overlap, observation-window confound, underpowering; partial concessions on each
cases.key_evidence: Four to five independently modest critiques across all runs (bidirectional look-ahead, host-overlap constraint ambiguity, observation-window confound, power/underpowering, label-construction correlation). Defenders partially concede each. Section 4 has an explicit confound-control model and offers 'Bidirectional LSTM or Transformer encoder' as alternatives — neither is cited by defenders. Adjudicator aggregates partial concessions to reach verdict.

cases.domain: cybersecurity SOC alert triage / learning-to-rank
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — Section 2 explicitly justifies stratification and temporal split rationale; defenders treat it as unresolved tension
cases.key_evidence: Section 2 explicitly states severity stratification is intentional to ensure balanced evaluation across alert types, and explains the temporal split rationale. Critics attack within-split stratification as contradicting temporal split purpose; defenders treat this as unresolved tension rather than citing Section 2's explicit design rationale for both choices. Defender concedes without citing the design's stated intent.

cases.domain: e-commerce / subscription churn prediction, Transformer vs GBM
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — survivorship, ablation degeneracy, recall calibration, sequence truncation; all 3 runs ETA
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs produce ETA verdict via accumulation of 4–5 modest critiques: cohort survivorship in new-user subgroup, Transformer-on-aggregates ablation degeneracy, recall@fixed-alert-rate calibration confound, sequence truncation asymmetry. Defenders partially concede each without any single critique being individually fatal. The new-user test section is explicitly specified in the temporal split design but defenders accept critic's cohort reframing.

cases.domain: fintech / card-not-present fraud detection, Transformer vs GBM
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — chargeback censoring, sequence context, metric conflation, feedback loop; all 3 runs ETA
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs produce ETA. Critics raise 4 critiques each: chargeback label censoring, sequence construction encoding ongoing-fraud context, metric conflation (PR-AUC vs recall@FPR), feedback loop handling. Section 3 specifies censored-label exclusion; Section 4 specifies orthogonal feature sets. Defenders partially concede each critique; no single critique is individually fatal given design controls. Accumulation drives verdict.

cases.domain: cybersecurity / temporal GNN for SOC incident triage
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — Section 3 no-post-alert exclusion and Section 4 Transformer baseline never cited against SIEM enrichment critique
cases.key_evidence: Section 3 explicitly states 'No post-alert information (analyst actions, subsequent incidents) is included' as a design control against label leakage. Critics raise SIEM retroactive enrichment leakage; defenders concede without citing this exclusion language. Section 4 includes a secondary Transformer baseline to isolate graph structure value — defenders fail to cite this when critics attack graph-structure attribution. Graph topology prerequisite critique has Mode 5 character (unfalsifiable) but Mode 3 citeable defenses drive the primary classification.

cases.domain: e-commerce / learning-to-rank for product search
cases.failure_mode_label: Partial concession — hypothesis tests reranker over BM25 candidates; defender conceded to 'must replace BM25 retrieval entirely' reframing
cases.key_evidence: Hypothesis claims LTR 'outperforms a baseline ranker using only lexical BM25' on NDCG@10 — a reranking claim consistent with the shared candidate pool design (Section 6). Critic reframes this as requiring LTR to replace BM25 retrieval entirely. Defender immediately concedes: 'I must concede the core point. The LTR model is a reranker, not a retrieval system.' Section 8 provides explicit long-tail ablation and popularity-bias analysis addressing the interaction-sparsity concern — never cited. Mode 2 primary, secondary Mode 3 evidence.

cases.domain: NLP / attention-based explanation faithfulness, replication study
cases.failure_mode_label: Full concession — defenders concede outright in 2/3 runs; run2 shows possible Mode 4 (prose defense wins, verdict=critique_wins anyway)
cases.key_evidence: Run0: full concession — 'I cannot adequately rebut given the methodology as described.' Run1: multiple critical flaws conceded including full concession on synthetic seq2seq tasks. Run2 is a notable exception: defender argues 'primary conclusion survives' and 'scoped limitations rather than critical flaws,' yet verdict=critique_wins — probable Mode 4 signal (correct rebuttal, pipeline verdict overrides). Majority pattern is Mode 1. Defenders in runs 0–1 fail to deploy the replication-study framing (replication methodology vs original causal claims).

cases.domain: XAI / generative causal explanations, replication study
cases.failure_mode_label: Full concession — all 3 runs concede original paper's causal claims without deploying replication-study framing
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs: defenders fully concede causal framing claims without deploying the replication-study defense. Critics attack original paper's methodology (VAE unidentifiability per Locatello et al., circular metrics, too-easy benchmarks). 6–7 issues per run, all adjudicated as uphold. Defenders never distinguish 'replication of experiment was successful' from 'original paper's causal claims are valid.' Given genuine methodological concerns about VAE unidentifiability, human review warranted to confirm case labeling is correct.

cases.domain: deep learning / nondeterminism and training instability, replication study
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — conditional vs marginal effects, mechanistic attribution, BatchEnsemble conflation; partial concessions across all runs
cases.key_evidence: 4–5 critiques per run: conditional vs marginal effect measurement, mechanistic attribution untestability (Mode 5 candidate), BatchEnsemble/TTA conflation of training vs inference variance, generalizability concerns. Defenders partially concede each: BatchEnsemble cost framing in runs 0 and 2, mechanistic attribution in run 1. No single critique individually fatal. Mode 5 character of the mechanistic attribution critique amplifies accumulation toward ETA/CW outcomes across runs.

cases.domain: XAI / label-free explainability for unsupervised models, replication study
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — evaluation circularity, small-N Pearson correlation, Hessian assumption; partial concessions accumulate to critique_wins in 2/3 runs
cases.key_evidence: 4–7 critiques per run: evaluation circularity (no external ground truth), Pearson correlation over very small model-configuration N, influence function Hessian assumption in non-convex unsupervised landscapes, no baseline comparison. Run0: partial concessions yield ETA. Runs 1–2: accumulation reaches critique_wins. Defenders never deploy replication framing (empirical results vs theoretical validity of methods). Technical concerns about Hessian and circular evaluation are genuine; human review warranted.

cases.domain: Image denoising — replication of SADNet spatial-adaptive CNN denoising architecture
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — deformable-conv mechanism, AWGN-only eval, real-noise dataset gaps; partial concessions across 5-6 issues per run
cases.key_evidence: 5–6 issues raised per run (deformable-conv mechanism unverified, AWGN-only synthetic eval, missing iso-parameter ablation, real-noise dataset exchangeability, metric adequacy). Run 0: defender explicitly labeled Issue 1 'a valid and important critique,' Issue 2 'a valid critique,' Issue 3 'a genuine gap.' Run 1: conceded deformable-conv mechanism 'largely valid.' Run 2: partial concessions on all 6 issues. No single issue was fatal but adjudicator aggregated cumulative concessions in all three runs. Defenders never pivoted to the replication-scope frame.

cases.domain: Explainability — replication of counterfactual group-explanation method in low-dimensional representations
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — design scopes faithfulness to smooth locally-invertible encoders; defenders engaged head-on with theoretical critique instead
cases.key_evidence: Task_prompt explicitly scopes the method as 'searches for changes in the original feature space that account for the observed separation' — a design constraint bounding where faithfulness validation is required. Defenders in all 3 runs conceded the VAE nonlinearity / inverse-faithfulness gap as 'a substantive and largely valid concern' rather than citing the design's explicit scope limitation. The correct rebuttal ('the method is scoped to settings where the encoder provides a smooth locally invertible mapping, as the paper states') was never used. Runs 0–1 reached ETA; Run 2 reached critique_wins after outright concession on re-encoding verification.

cases.domain: Remote sensing / crop classification — replication of PSE+TAE satellite time series classification
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — spatial topology loss, positional encoding ambiguity, spatial autocorrelation, incomplete ablation; all 3 runs ETA
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs reached empirical_test_agreed. 5–6 issues per run: spatial topology loss, positional encoding ambiguity (index vs. date), spatial autocorrelation in train/test splits, incomplete ablation space, single-region evaluation. Defenders mounted reasonable design-intent responses but partially conceded each issue — e.g., 'this is a real and well-reasoned concern' on boundary-pixel conflation, full concession on missing blocked-split evaluation. Adjudicator required empirical tests for Issues 3 and 4 in all 3 runs; no run reached defense_wins despite no single issue being decisive alone.

cases.domain: Optimization — replication of AdaBelief optimizer multi-domain evaluation
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — tuning asymmetry, GAN stability, PTB weakness, epsilon unexplored; concessions drive critique_wins in 2/3 runs
cases.key_evidence: 5–6 issues per run: hyperparameter tuning asymmetry, GAN stability unverifiable without multi-seed variance, mechanistic claim unsupported, PTB too weak a benchmark, epsilon load-bearing but unexplored, missing mechanism ablation. Run 0 defender: 'the paper does not provide formal documentation of hyperparameter search budgets.' Runs 1–2 reached critique_wins as concessions on tuning asymmetry, GAN stability, PTB, and mechanism attribution accumulated. No single critique was decisive — classic noise accumulation.

cases.domain: Generative models / interpretability — replication of GANSpace PCA-based GAN control discovery
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — weak random-direction baseline, PCA-interpretability conflation, linearity assumption; Run 1 critique_wins, others ETA
cases.key_evidence: 4–5 issues per run: trivially weak random-direction baseline (no SeFa/ICA/InterFaceGAN comparison), PCA-variance vs. interpretability conflation, insufficient sample count for high-dim PCA, linearity assumption unjustified for BigGAN, layer-wise hierarchy conflated with StyleGAN inductive bias. Defenders in all 3 runs conceded baseline weakness as 'substantially valid' and PCA-interpretability alignment as a genuine unverified assumption. Run 1 reached critique_wins; Runs 0 and 2 reached ETA. Aggregate of partial concessions on 4–5 issues drove all verdicts.

cases.domain: NLP / fairness — replication of gender bias mitigation in fantasy role-playing dialogue (LIGHT)
cases.failure_mode_label: Partial concession — defenders accepted original-paper-validity scope instead of replication-soundness scope
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs reached critique_wins. Dominant Issue 1 across all runs: evaluation target (LIGHT ground truth) is itself male-biased, making 'matching LIGHT distribution' a structurally invalid bias-reduction metric. Defenders in all 3 runs conceded directly: 'this is a valid and important critique,' 'I need to be honest here.' No defender invoked the replication-scope frame: 'the replication correctly reproduced the original paper's methodology — whether that methodology adequately operationalizes bias reduction is a critique of the original paper, not of the replication study.' case_valid=false: the core evaluation metric is structurally invalid for the stated goal even within the original paper.

cases.domain: Self-supervised learning theory — replication of BYOL/SimSiam dynamics analysis and DirectPred method
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — linearized-dynamics assumption, linear probing insufficiency, DirectPred degeneration, SGD baseline; all 3 runs ETA
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs reached empirical_test_agreed. 4–5 issues per run: linearized-dynamics assumption doesn't hold for deep networks, linear probing insufficient to validate eigenspace claims, DirectPred may degenerate to identity on low-rank representations, SGD baseline potentially misconfigured, EMA coefficient ablation conflated. Defenders conceded each issue as 'genuine and important' while providing standard theory-vs-practice hedges. Accumulation of unresolved mechanistic gaps across 4–5 issues prevented defense_wins in all runs despite no single fatal flaw.

cases.domain: Bandits — replication of Thompson sampling for clustered-arm stochastic and contextual bandits
cases.failure_mode_label: Weak rebuttal — correctly argued known-cluster scope on Issue 1 but didn't sustain the argument through missing-baseline concession
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs reached critique_wins. Defenders correctly identified the scope defense for Issue 1 (oracle cluster assignments): 'design intent is explicitly to study the known-cluster setting — this is common and legitimate in structured bandit literature.' This was the correct rebuttal. However, defenders then conceded Issue 3 (missing naive clustered Thompson sampling baseline) without recognizing that the same scope argument applies: the comparison is against cluster-unaware baselines precisely to measure the value of exploiting known structure. Correct defense required sustaining the scope argument through all empirical comparisons; defenders yielded instead.

cases.domain: Algorithmic fairness — replication of fairness/efficiency in online secretary/prophet selection problems
cases.failure_mode_label: Unfalsifiable critique — fairness definition post-hoc, efficiency-gap conflation, real-world dataset distribution violations
cases.key_evidence: Verdicts split: ETA (Run 0), critique_wins (Runs 1–2). Dominant critiques are unfalsifiable: 'fairness definition may be chosen post-hoc to satisfy the algorithm,' 'efficiency gap conflates classical online/offline price with cost of fairness,' 'UFRGS real-world dataset violates uniform-permutation assumption.' Run 0's defender correctly argued the fairness criterion predates this paper and is inherited from prior literature — the strongest single-issue rebuttal in the dataset — yet still reached ETA due to UFRGS distribution concerns. Runs 1–2 conceded the circularity concern too broadly.

cases.domain: Neural network pruning — replication of learning rate rewinding vs. fine-tuning comparison study
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — rewind-point asymmetry, compute-budget confound, CIFAR-10 saturation, optimizer state; all 3 runs ETA
cases.key_evidence: All 3 runs reached empirical_test_agreed. 3–5 issues per run: rewind-point hyperparameter asymmetry, compute budget confound between LR rewinding and fine-tuning, CIFAR-10 saturation compressing effect sizes, optimizer internal state not controlled, magnitude-pruning mask/weight inconsistency. Defenders mounted the strongest rebuttals of any case (particularly on Issue 1: 'shared rewind point applied identically to both rewinding methods') but still conceded compute-budget confound and CIFAR-10 generalizability. Cumulative effect of 3–5 partial concessions produced ETA in all runs.

cases.domain: Ride-pooling optimization — replication of fairness/efficiency objective comparison via MDP dispatching
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — tautological fairness evaluation, fare-heterogeneity proxy, neighborhood MAUP sensitivity; ETA (Run 0), critique_wins (Runs 1-2)
cases.key_evidence: Verdicts: ETA (Run 0), critique_wins (Runs 1–2). 5 issues per run: value estimator training entangled with evaluation objective, neighborhood boundary MAUP sensitivity, success-rate profitability proxy invalid (ignores fare heterogeneity), near-tautological fairness evaluation (objectives evaluated on their own metric), income normalization missing. Run 2 explicitly conceded Issue 1 (near-tautological evaluation) as 'a clean logical flaw.' The fare-heterogeneity proxy issue was conceded in Runs 1–2 as 'partially valid.' Accumulation of genuine-but-not-fatal partial concessions drove non-defense verdicts.

cases.domain: Robotics / value alignment — replication of minimal-questionnaire value alignment verification framework
cases.failure_mode_label: Unfalsifiable critique — adversarial threat model undefined, false-negative rates on non-adversarial robots only, low-dimensional gridworld scalability
cases.key_evidence: Verdicts: ETA (Runs 0 and 2), critique_wins (Run 1). Dominant critiques are structurally unfalsifiable without new formal proofs: adversarial threat model undefined for certification claims, false negative rates measured on random (not adversarially-constructed) misaligned robots, low-dimensional gridworld evaluation may not indicate scalability. Runs 0 and 2 defenders correctly defended adversarial-robustness scope ('paper studies verification, not adversarial robustness — scope limitation, not a flaw under stated threat model') but still yielded on overall accumulation. Run 1 conceded certification framing too broadly, reaching critique_wins.

cases.domain: Motion prediction / social navigation — replication of Social-NCE contrastive learning for trajectory prediction
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — oracle negative construction, social-norm operationalization, metric decoupling, stale negatives; critique_wins (Run 0), ETA (Runs 1-2)
cases.key_evidence: Verdicts: critique_wins (Run 0), ETA (Runs 1–2). 5–6 issues per run: oracle ground-truth dependency in negative construction, geometric-proximity-only operationalization of social norms, collision/FDE metric decoupling, stale negatives in RL extension, missing non-social contrastive control. Run 0 defender partially conceded Issues 1, 2, and 5, explicitly noting Issue 5 (non-social contrastive baseline missing) 'cannot be argued away.' Runs 1–2 made similar partial concessions across 5+ issues but held at ETA. Accumulation of partial concessions without any decisive single failure drove all verdicts.

cases.domain: 3D-aware face generation — replication of LiftedGAN (StyleGAN2 distillation to 3D-aware framework)
cases.failure_mode_label: Noise accumulation — entangled distillation target, Lambertian lighting baking, FID insensitivity, bas-relief ambiguity; critique_wins (Runs 0,2), ETA (Run 1)
cases.key_evidence: Verdicts: critique_wins (Runs 0 and 2), ETA (Run 1). 5–6 issues per run: entangled distillation target (StyleGAN2 latents don't encode disentangled 3D), simplified Lambertian lighting bakes shading into texture, FID insensitive to disentanglement quality, bas-relief depth ambiguity from 2D-only supervision, foreground mask correlated with pose. Defenders in all 3 runs conceded Issues 1 and 2 as 'substantially correct' and 'real limitations.' Run 1 defender mounted strongest rebuttal (rendering pipeline as inductive bias for disentanglement) and landed at ETA. Runs 0 and 2 reached critique_wins as concessions on 3–4 issues accumulated.
